mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in nonfarm payrolls above 100k signals robust labor market conditions that boost corporate earnings and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are negative in 60%+ of instances where PAYEMS exceeds 100k, or the signal's IC < 0.05 in rolling 1-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A concurrent decline in both consumer price inflation (CPI) and housing starts (HOUST) signals weak demand conditions that reduce corporate pricing power, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns are positive in > 50% of cases where both CPIAUCSL and HOUST decline, or the interaction term's coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly rise in industrial production (INDPRO) above its 3-month moving average indicates strengthening manufacturing activity that improves corporate margins, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are negative in 60%+ of instances where INDPRO exceeds its 3-month MA, or the signal's IC < 0.04 in rolling 1-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A 12-month decline in the monetary base (RSAFS) combined with rising interest rates (FEDFUNDS) signals a tightening cycle that increases equity risk premiums, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns are positive in > 50% of cases where RSAFS declines and FEDFUNDS rises, or the interaction term's coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate (UNRATE) decline below 5.0% signals a tight labor market that increases wage pressures and corporate costs, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are positive in 60%+ of instances where UNRATE < 5.0%, or the signal's IC < 0.03 in rolling 1-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A concurrent rise in consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) and housing starts (HOUST) signals strong demand conditions that increase corporate pricing power, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are negative in 60%+ of cases where both CPIAUCSL and HOUST rise, or the interaction term's coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in industrial production (INDPRO) combined with rising unemployment (UNRATE) signals a manufacturing slowdown that reduces corporate earnings, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns are positive in > 50% of cases where INDPRO declines and UNRATE rises, or the interaction term's coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.1).
